result.stdout:
Framework: .NET 5.0.17
livingdoc:
  A dotnet tool for SpecFlow to generate a shareable HTML Gherkin feature execution report (living documentation). Use together with SpecFlow.Plus.LivingDocPlugin.

Usage:
  livingdoc [options] [command]

Options:
  --version         Show version information
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  feature-folder <feature-folder>
  test-assembly <test-assembly>
  feature-data <feature-data-json>

commands.payload:
Framework: .NET 5.0.17
livingdoc:
  A dotnet tool for SpecFlow to generate a shareable HTML Gherkin feature execution report (living documentation). Use together with SpecFlow.Plus.LivingDocPlugin.

Usage:
  livingdoc [options] [command]

Options:
  --version         Show version information
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  feature-folder <feature-folder>
  test-assembly <test-assembly>
  feature-data <feature-data-json>

result.stdout:
Framework: .NET 5.0.17
Usage:
  livingdoc feature-data [options] <feature-data-json>

Arguments:
  <feature-data-json>    Relative or absolute path to the feature data json

Options:
  -t, --test-execution-json <test-execution-json>      Relative or absolute path to the test execution result JSON files. Relative GLOB pattern-matching is supported.
  --title <title>                                      The header of the generated Living Documentation.
  --work-item-url-template <work-item-url-template>    URL template to use to generate the work item links. e.g: https://dev.azure.com/fabrikam/FabrikamProj/_workitems/edit/{id}
  --work-item-prefix <work-item-prefix>                Special tag you mark the scenarios with to link them to the respecting work items
  --binding-assemblies <binding-assemblies>            Relative or absolute path to the binding assemblies.
  -o, --output <output>                                Relative or absolute path to the output.
  --output-type <HTML|JSON>                            Type of the output file.
  --include-test-output <All|None>                     Include outputs in the FeatureData.json.
  -?, -h, --help                                       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Framework: .NET 5.0.17
Usage:
  livingdoc feature-folder [options] <feature-folder>

Arguments:
  <feature-folder>    Relative or absolute path to the feature Folder

Options:
  --project-language <project-language>                Language of the feature file.
  -t, --test-execution-json <test-execution-json>      Relative or absolute path to the test execution result JSON files. Relative GLOB pattern-matching is supported.
  --title <title>                                      The header of the generated Living Documentation.
  --project-name <project-name>                        This name is used by the root node in the tree. If you do not enter a name here, the name of the feature folder is used by default
  --work-item-url-template <work-item-url-template>    URL template to use to generate the work item links. e.g: https://dev.azure.com/fabrikam/FabrikamProj/_workitems/edit/{id}
  --work-item-prefix <work-item-prefix>                Special tag you mark the scenarios with to link them to the respecting work items
  --binding-assemblies <binding-assemblies>            Relative or absolute path to the binding assemblies.
  -o, --output <output>                                Relative or absolute path to the output.
  --output-type <HTML|JSON>                            Type of the output file.
  --include-test-output <All|None>                     Include outputs in the FeatureData.json.
  -?, -h, --help                                       Show help and usage information

  --title <title>                                      The header of the generated Living Documentation.
  --project-name <project-name>                        This name is used by the root node in the tree. If you do not enter a name here, the name of the test assembly is used by default
  --work-item-url-template <work-item-url-template>    URL template to use to generate the work item links. e.g: https://dev.azure.com/fabrikam/FabrikamProj/_workitems/edit/{id}
  --work-item-prefix <work-item-prefix>                Special tag you mark the scenarios with to link them to the respecting work items

  -o, --output <output>                                Relative or absolute path to the output.
  --output-type <HTML|JSON>                            Type of the output file.
  --include-test-output <All|None>                     Include outputs in the FeatureData.json.
  -?, -h, --help                                       Show help and usage information
